notes: Roman secretary and nobleman involved in a revolutionary movement; guardian of a Christian woman related to Gregory of Nazianzus.

docs.title: BOOK XVII
docs.author: Ammianus Marcellinus
docs.excerpt: … who afterwards, under the pressure of violent necessity, committed himself to a revolutionary movement. +Enosichthôn+, +Seisichthôn+, +Ennosigdaios+, from +enothô+ and +seiô+, to shake, and +chthan+ and +gaia+, the earth. From +brazô+, to boil over.

docs.title: BOOK XXVI
docs.author: Ammianus Marcellinus
docs.excerpt: He was very strict in his way of life and morals, reserved and silent; but both as secretary, and afterwards as tribune distinguishing himself by his services in war, and rising gradually to the highest rank.

docs.title: BOOK XXIX
docs.author: Ammianus Marcellinus
docs.excerpt: 5. A certain Procopius, a restless man, at all times covetous and fond of disturbances, had persuaded Anatolius and Spudasius, officers about the palace, who had been ordered to restore what they had appropriated from the treasury, to bring a plot against the Count Fortunatianus,
